collection.description: The shelf in the front room and the stack by the reading chair. Chess, bees, the old country, and the quiet ones I read at night.

book.title: My System
book.author: Aron Nimzowitsch
book.note: The blockade chapter is dog eared. I teach from this on tactics nights.

book.title: Dvoretsky's Endgame Manual
book.author: Mark Dvoretsky
book.note: The rook endgames. The Ostrowski draw would not have slipped if I had finished chapter nine.

book.title: Zurich International Chess Tournament 1953
book.author: David Bronstein
book.note: Best annotations ever written. Read it twice.

book.title: The Hive and the Honey Bee
book.author: editorial collective
book.note: The one I hand to anyone starting out.

book.title: Seasonal Management for the Northeast Apiary
book.author: regional guild press
book.note: Margins full of my own notes on the twelve colonies.

book.title: Kobzar
book.author: Taras Shevchenko
book.note: Was my father's copy. The binding is going. I will not have it rebound by a stranger.

book.title: Boa Constrictor and Other Stories
book.author: Ivan Franko
book.note: Brought from Kharkiv in 1996. One of the few things in the suitcase.

book.title: Tinker Tailor Soldier Spy
book.author: John le Carre
book.note: Smiley thinks like an endgame player. Patience, then one move.

book.title: The Spy Who Came in from the Cold
book.author: John le Carre
book.note: Read it on the night shifts during the apprenticeship.

item.title: My System
item.note: Told him if he wants to be sharp on a panel he can learn to be sharp on a board.
